title: Further Product Compression On Repeated Pressure
goal: Resolve concrete product-compression friction in #319 by making the llms.txt generated-adapter refresh path truthful.
If scope remains small, resolve #326 by splitting durable rationale out of the recurring-friction runbook into a decision note.
non_goals: Do not rewrite the full startup model.
Do not delete weak-agent routing surfaces without replacement.
Do not close #230 unless the broader product-shape direction is materially complete.
Do not make llms.txt a richer handbook.

intent.outcome: Visible compatibility residue is smaller and more trustworthy: llms.txt has one working refresh path, and recurring friction memory separates procedure from rationale.
intent.constraints: Prefer deletion/demotion/automation over adding new visible prose; preserve compact startup routing.
intent.latitude: Choose whether #319 alone is the milestone or whether #326 fits the same bounded product-compression proof.
intent.escalation: Stop if work expands into broad front-door redesign or deletes compatibility surfaces.

execution.next_step: Archive this slice while keeping the product-compression lane open for #230/#281.
execution.proof: Changing the handoff source has one documented refresh path and the maintainer target leaves no adapter drift.

invariants: llms.txt stays lightweight and generated-adapter shaped.
make maintainer-surfaces is the truthful refresh command when llms.txt source changes.
Memory runbooks stay procedural and compact.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: product-shape compression by removing or demoting visible residue

required_continuation.activation trigger: generated adapter refresh remains untruthful, memory rationale remains in procedural runbooks, or broader #230 remains materially open

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: A concrete compatibility adapter can be maintained through one command rather than manual patching.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Broad startup/front-door deletion or generated-doc family redesign.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: make maintainer-surfaces needed a real llms.txt render step; recurring-friction rationale can live in a decision note while the runbook stays procedural.

intent_interpretation.literal request: execute all planned work lane by lane and dogfood the package
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: remove real visible-product residue that repeated dogfooding has proven costly
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: make llms.txt refresh automatic/truthful and split recurring-friction rationale if bounded
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject changes that add more visible docs or broaden llms.txt

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: ship concrete product-compression cleanup for generated adapter refresh and, if bounded, memory rationale split
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: preserve compact routing; do not broaden llms.txt; do not delete weak-agent compatibility surfaces
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: script/helper shape, tests, and whether #326 fits the same bounded milestone
delegated_judgment.escalate when: the work requires front-door redesign, broad generated-doc removal, or changing startup semantics

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Makefile, scripts, workspace CLI helper/tests, maintainer docs if command list changes, memory recurring-friction runbook/decision/manifest if #326 is included, active planning state and execplan
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_maintainer_surfaces.py -q -k "llms or maintainer or external_agent_handoff or recurring"; make maintainer-surfaces; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before deleting entry surfaces or rewriting startup/front-door doctrine

context_budget.live working set: #319, #326, #230, Makefile maintainer target, llms handoff source, recurring-friction memory surfaces, active execplan
context_budget.recoverable later: full product-compression history and older startup/front-door archive plans
context_budget.externalize before shift: update this execplan execution_run and immediate_next_action
context_budget.pre-work config pull: uv run agentic-workspace summary and gh issue view for #319/#326/#230
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: read only the recurring-friction runbook, candidate decision note, and manifest if #326 is included
context_budget.tiny resumability note: make llms.txt refresh truthful through make maintainer-surfaces; split memory rationale only if bounded
context_budget.context-shift triggers: refresh path requires startup semantics changes or memory cleanup expands beyond recurring-friction rationale

stop_conditions.stop when: #319 and any included bounded memory cleanup are shipped, dogfooded, committed, pushed, and closed or residual work is routed
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: a generated adapter cannot be refreshed without changing lifecycle semantics
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: work becomes a broad product-shape redesign
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: make maintainer-surfaces still leaves llms.txt drift

active_milestone.scope: maintainer refresh truth plus bounded memory hygiene if it stays small

immediate_next_action: Archive this slice while preserving the product-compression roadmap lane.
invariants: Changing truth stays in structured/queryable sources.
Procedural memory runbooks do not accumulate rationale.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_maintainer_surfaces.py -q -k "llms or maintainer or external_agent_handoff or recurring"
completion_criteria: Changing the external-agent handoff template has one documented refresh path.
make maintainer-surfaces updates llms.txt or the authority marker no longer claims it does.
Doctor/report no longer reports adapter drift after the documented refresh command has run.
Any recurring-friction rationale cleanup leaves the runbook procedural and manifest-routed.

execution_run.what happened: Added a source-owned llms.txt renderer, wired make maintainer-surfaces and strict maintainer-surfaces to run it before drift checks, added tests for stale/current llms rendering, and split recurring-friction rationale from the procedural runbook into a manifest-routed decision note.
execution_run.scope touched: Makefile maintainer target, workspace CLI handoff helper, render script, maintainer tests, recurring-friction memory runbook/decision/manifest, and active planning state
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_maintainer_surfaces.py -q -k "llms or maintainer or external_agent_handoff or recurring"; uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/cli.py scripts/render_external_agent_handoff.py tests/test_maintainer_surfaces.py; make maintainer-surfaces; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --format json; uv run pytest -q; uv run ruff check src tests scripts; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_run.result for continuation: #319 and #326 are complete; #230 and #281 remain open as the broader product-compression continuation.

proof_report.validation proof: Focused tests, full pytest, ruff, maintainer-surfaces, report dogfood, planning checker, and diff check passed; diff check only reported existing line-ending warnings.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: make maintainer-surfaces now runs render_external_agent_handoff before drift checks; tests prove stale llms.txt is updated and current llms.txt remains current; report no longer shows the recurring-friction rationale/manual-review warning.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Reduce product-visible residue proven costly by repeated dogfooding.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: #319 and #326 concrete friction are satisfied: the llms adapter refresh path is truthful, and recurring-friction rationale is split into a decision note.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Reduce visible product residue while preserving durable leverage and clear ownership.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Prefer making an existing command truthful over adding another visible instruction surface.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this remove a repeated first-line friction without increasing startup concepts?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #319 and #326 landed, but #230/#281 still represent broader product-compression and generated-compatibility residue decisions.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: this execplan proof_report plus open #230/#281 and the roadmap lane
closure_check.reopen trigger: llms.txt refresh remains manual or memory runbook rationale keeps accumulating

execution_summary.outcome delivered: The concrete #319/#326 friction is resolved while the larger product-compression lane remains open.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: focused tests, full pytest, ruff, maintainer-surfaces, report dogfood, planning checker, and diff check
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: recurring-friction decision note and manifest route
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): Memory decision note added; no config changes needed.

drift_log: 2026-04-26: Promoted product compression lane with #319 as the concrete first milestone and #326 as a bounded secondary cleanup if scope remains small.
